description: Template Service Phase 5 — Integration + Contract Tests: shared test helpers, full CRUD + lifecycle integration tests against real Postgres, render integration tests (happy paths, cache eviction, edge cases), and TypeBox contract tests for the render endpoint.

userStories.title: Integration test helpers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want shared test infrastructure so that integration tests can spin up a real Fastify app against a test Postgres database, generate auth tokens, and reset state between runs.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `test/integration/helpers.ts`
Export `TEST_JWT_SECRET = 'integration-test-secret-32-chars-ok!'`
Export `TEST_DB_URL` sourced from `process.env['DATABASE_URL']` with fallback `'postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/postgres'`

Export `async function makeStaffToken(sub = 'user-staff'): Promise<string>` — signs a JWT with `{ sub, roles: ['marketing_staff'] }` using HS256 and TEST_JWT_SECRET via `SignJWT` from `jose`; token expires in 1 hour
Export `async function makeManagerToken(sub = 'user-manager'): Promise<string>` — signs a JWT with `{ sub, roles: ['marketing_manager'] }` same way
Export `function makeServiceApiKey(): string` — returns `'ak_test_service_key'` (string starting with `ak_` to satisfy `isServiceApiKey` check in auth plugin)

Export `async function resetSchema(db: Knex): Promise<void>` — drops `platform_templates` schema with `db.raw('DROP SCHEMA IF EXISTS platform_templates CASCADE')`, then calls the `up` function from `../../migrations/001_create_templates.js` and `../../migrations/002_create_template_versions.js`
Export `async function truncateTables(db: Knex): Promise<void>` — truncates `platform_templates.template_versions` first (FK child), then `platform_templates.templates` using `TRUNCATE ... CASCADE`
Export interface `TestContext { app: FastifyInstance; db: Knex; serverUrl: string; close: () => Promise<void> }`

Export `async function createTestContext(): Promise<TestContext>` — creates a Knex instance connected to TEST_DB_URL, calls `buildApp(db, TEST_JWT_SECRET)` from `../../src/app.js`, starts the app on `{ port: 0, host: '127.0.0.1' }`, returns context with `serverUrl` derived from `app.server.address()`; `close()` calls `app.close()` then `db.destroy()`
Import `buildApp` from `../../src/app.js`
Import `knex` from `knex` for the Knex instance; connection config: `{ client: 'pg', connection: TEST_DB_URL }`
No Redis dependencies — template service has none
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The JWT secret used in test helpers MUST match the one passed to `buildApp` — both use TEST_JWT_SECRET. The `makeStaffToken` and `makeManagerToken` functions use `new TextEncoder().encode(TEST_JWT_SECRET)` as the key (not createSecretKey from crypto) — this matches how `src/plugins/auth.ts` verifies tokens using jose's `jwtVerify` with `new TextEncoder().encode(secret)`. The service API key check in auth.ts is: `token.startsWith('ak_')` — so any string starting with 'ak_' works; use it as `Authorization: Bearer ak_test_service_key`. The `resetSchema` function imports migration `up` functions using relative ESM paths with `.js` extension; the actual file on disk is `.ts` but TypeScript resolves `.js` imports to `.ts` source files in the project's ESM configuration.

userStories.title: CRUD + lifecycle integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for all CRUD and lifecycle operations against a real Postgres database so that the full template management API is verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `test/integration/templates-crud.test.ts`
Use `beforeAll` to call `createTestContext()` and `resetSchema(ctx.db)`
Use `beforeEach` to call `truncateTables(ctx.db)` so each test starts with clean tables
Use `afterAll` to call `ctx.close()`
All HTTP requests use `fetch` against `ctx.serverUrl` with `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header using `await makeStaffToken()` unless otherwise noted
POST /templates with `{ name: 'Welcome SMS', channel: 'sms' }` → 201 with `{ status: 'draft', current_version: 1, active_version: null, channel: 'sms' }`
POST /templates with duplicate name → 409 `{ error: 'Template name already exists' }`
GET /templates returns `{ data: [...], total: N, limit: 20, offset: 0 }`; create 3 templates, verify `total === 3` and `data.length === 3`
GET /templates?channel=sms returns only SMS templates (create 1 SMS + 1 email, filter returns 1)
GET /templates?status=active returns only active templates; create 2 templates, activate one, filter returns 1
GET /templates?sort=created_at&order=asc returns templates in ascending creation order (create 2, verify order by created_at)
GET /templates/:id returns group row with `draft_content` (version object) and `active_content: null` when never activated
GET /templates/:id after activation returns `active_content` populated with the active version's body fields

PATCH /templates/:id on never-activated template: set `body_text: 'Hello {{name}}'`, verify response has `draft_content.body_text === 'Hello {{name}}'`, `current_version === 1` (in-place update, version unchanged)
PATCH /templates/:id on active template with no pending draft: first activate, then patch; verify `current_version === 2`, `active_version === 1`, `draft_content.body_text` has new value

PATCH /templates/:id on draft already in progress (current_version > active_version): patch twice without activating between; verify `current_version` stays at 2 on second patch (in-place update of draft)

PATCH /templates/:id on SMS template with email-only fields `{ subject: 'Test', body_html: '

Hi

' }` → 200 without error; subsequent GET shows `draft_content` has no subject/body_html stored
PATCH /templates/:id with `body_text` of 1601 chars on SMS template → 400
PATCH /templates/:id on disabled template → 200 (allowed); verify `status` remains `'disabled'` in response
POST /templates/:id/activate with manager token → 200; `active_version === current_version`, `status === 'active'`
POST /templates/:id/disable on active template with manager token → 200; `status === 'disabled'`
POST /templates/:id/disable on draft template (never activated, active_version is null) with manager token → 200 with `warning` field in response body
POST /templates/:id/disable on already-disabled template with manager token → 400 `{ error: 'Template is already disabled' }`
POST /templates/:id/enable on disabled template (with active_version) with manager token → 200; `status === 'active'`
POST /templates/:id/enable on non-disabled (active) template with manager token → 400 `{ error: 'Template is not disabled' }`
POST /templates/:id/activate with staff token (no manager role) → 403
POST /templates/:id/disable with staff token → 403
POST /templates/:id/enable with staff token → 403 (first disable with manager token to set up state)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use the global `fetch` (Node.js 24 has it built-in — no node-fetch needed). Each test in `templates-crud.test.ts` should be a top-level `it(...)` or grouped in `describe` blocks by operation. Keep tests independent — each beforeEach truncates tables so order doesn't matter. For the PATCH email-only-fields-on-SMS test: after patching an SMS template with subject/body_html, do a GET and check `draft_content` — the fields should be null/absent since the route silently ignores cross-channel fields. For the `body_text` limit test: generate a 1601-char string with `'a'.repeat(1601)`. The `makeManagerToken` and `makeStaffToken` helpers are async — await them in beforeAll or per-test as needed. For 'enable on non-disabled' test: the template should be in 'active' status (just activated, not disabled) — call enable on it and expect 400.

userStories.title: Render integration tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for the render endpoint so that template rendering, cache eviction behavior, and error cases are verified end-to-end against a real database.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `test/integration/render.test.ts`
Use `beforeAll` to call `createTestContext()` and `resetSchema(ctx.db)`

Use `beforeEach` to call `truncateTables(ctx.db)` AND call `templateCache.evict` for any template IDs used in the previous test — or use a unique template name per test to avoid cache bleed between tests
Use `afterAll` to call `ctx.close()`

Helper in the test file: `async function createAndActivateSmsTemplate(name: string, bodyText: string)` that POSTs to create a template, PATCHes body_text, then POSTs to activate; returns the template id
Helper: `async function createAndActivateEmailTemplate(name: string, opts: { subject: string; body_html: string; body_text: string })` same flow for email templates

Active SMS template render: create+activate SMS template with `body_text: 'Hi {{first_name}}!'`, POST /templates/render with `{ template_id, context: { first_name: 'Sarah' } }` → 200 `{ channel: 'sms', body_text: 'Hi Sarah!' }`

Active email template render: create+activate email template with subject `'Hello {{first_name}}'`, body_html `'

Hello {{first_name}}

'`, body_text `'Hello {{first_name}}'`; render with context `{ first_name: 'Sarah' }` → 200 `{ channel: 'email', subject: 'Hello Sarah', body_html: '

Hello Sarah

', body_text: 'Hello Sarah' }`
SMS render response contains only `channel` and `body_text` (no `subject` or `body_html` fields)
Template with `active_version IS NULL` (draft, never activated) → render → 404 `{ error: 'Template not found or not renderable' }`
Template with `status = disabled` → render → 404 `{ error: 'Template not found or not renderable' }` (disable after activating, eviction is eager)
Draft edit does not affect render: activate v1, render → get v1 content, PATCH (creates v2 draft), render again → still returns v1 content (active_version unchanged)
Activate evicts cache and subsequent render returns new content: activate v1, render (caches v1), PATCH + activate (v2 now active, cache evicted), render → returns v2 content (not cached v1)
Disable evicts cache: activate, render (populates cache), disable, render immediately → 404 (eager eviction, not waiting for TTL)
Enable allows render after disable: activate, disable, enable, render → 200 with content
Missing context keys → empty strings: render with `context: {}` on template with `body_text: 'Hi {{first_name}}!'` → 200 `{ body_text: 'Hi !' }` (missing key replaced with empty string)
Malformed merge tag in stored content: create template, PATCH with `body_text: 'Hi {{ }}'` (empty tag), activate, render → 400 `{ error: 'Malformed merge tag in template content' }`
Unauthenticated render request (no Authorization header) → 401
Service API key auth: render with `Authorization: Bearer ak_test_service_key` on active template → 200 (no role required)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Cache isolation between tests: since the template cache is a module-level singleton shared across the app instance, rendering a template in one test populates the cache. The cleanest approach is to import `templateCache` from `../../src/services/template-cache.js` in the test file and call `templateCache.evict(templateId)` in afterEach or at the start of each test that uses a known template ID. Alternatively, since each beforeEach truncates tables and each test creates new templates with `gen_random_uuid()` IDs, cache entries from deleted templates are harmless (the stale cache entry would just cause a DB lookup returning null on the next get, which is a cache miss that returns null — safe). For the 'malformed merge tag' test: the PATCH endpoint stores whatever body_text is given (it does not validate merge tag syntax at write time per the spec); only the render endpoint validates. The `{{ }}` empty tag (with spaces) should pass the PATCH but fail at render time. Note: the spec says PATCH should catch malformed tags at save time only as a note, but the design spec section 5.1 says malformed tags cause 400 at render time; the PATCH handler in phases 1-3 does NOT validate merge tag syntax.

userStories.title: Contract tests for render endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I want TypeBox schema contract tests for the render endpoint so that request and response shapes are explicitly validated and serve as living documentation for consumers.
acceptanceCriteria: Create `test/contract/render-contract.test.ts`
Import `Type, Static` from `@sinclair/typebox` and `Value` from `@sinclair/typebox/value` for runtime validation

Define TypeBox schema `RenderRequestSchema = Type.Object({ template_id: Type.String(), context: Type.Record(Type.String(), Type.Unknown()) })` and verify a valid request object `{ template_id: 'some-uuid', context: { first_name: 'Sarah' } }` passes `Value.Check(RenderRequestSchema, input) === true`
Verify invalid request (missing `template_id`) fails schema check: `Value.Check(RenderRequestSchema, { context: {} }) === false`
Verify invalid request (missing `context`) fails schema check: `Value.Check(RenderRequestSchema, { template_id: 'uuid' }) === false`

Define TypeBox schema `SmsRenderResponseSchema = Type.Object({ channel: Type.Literal('sms'), body_text: Type.String() })` and verify `{ channel: 'sms', body_text: 'Hi Sarah!' }` passes; verify object with extra fields still passes (TypeBox default is open schemas); verify `{ channel: 'email', body_text: 'x' }` fails (wrong literal)

Define TypeBox schema `EmailRenderResponseSchema = Type.Object({ channel: Type.Literal('email'), subject: Type.String(), body_html: Type.String(), body_text: Type.String() })` and verify a valid email response object passes; verify missing `subject` fails
Define TypeBox schema `ErrorResponseSchema = Type.Object({ error: Type.String() })` and verify `{ error: 'Template not found or not renderable' }` passes; verify `{}` fails
All assertions use `expect(Value.Check(schema, input)).toBe(true/false)` — no live HTTP calls; purely schema validation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are pure in-memory schema validation tests — no database, no HTTP server needed. Import `Value` from `@sinclair/typebox/value` for runtime type checking (this is a sub-path export of the @sinclair/typebox package already in dependencies). The `Value.Check(schema, input)` function returns boolean — it validates the input against the TypeBox schema at runtime. No Pact or external contract framework — plain Vitest assertions. TypeBox schemas are the ground truth for what consumers (Automation Engine, Nurturing Engine, Campaign Service) can expect in render responses. If `@sinclair/typebox/value` import path does not resolve, try `import { Value } from '@sinclair/typebox/value'` — this is the standard sub-path export in typebox 0.34+.
